result:
Here's a backward-planning milestone plan. I'll anchor it to a target **launch date of mid-September 2026** (SaaStr Annual is typically mid-September — confirm the exact date and adjust). Working back from there, you have roughly **15–16 weeks** from today (May 30).

## Target: Launch at SaaStr — ~Sept 14, 2026

| Milestone | Target window | Why this timing |
|---|---|---|
| **🎯 GA Launch / SaaStr** | Sept 14 (week of) | The fixed anchor |
| **Launch readiness freeze** | Sept 7 | Code freeze; no new features, bugs only |
| **Beta → GA cutover ready** | Aug 31 | Onboarding, billing, support docs live |
| **Marketing assets locked** | Aug 24 | Booth, demo script, landing page, PR draft |
| **Closed beta with design partners** | Aug 3 – Aug 28 | ~4 weeks of real-usage feedback |
| **Feature complete** | Aug 1 | All v2 scope built; only polish/bugfix after |
| **Internal dogfooding / QA pass** | Jul 20 – Aug 1 | Catch issues before external eyes |
| **Core build sprint** | Jun 15 – Jul 31 | The main engineering push (~6 weeks) |
| **Scope lock + spec sign-off** | Jun 12 | Decide what's *in* v2 vs. fast-follow |
| **Kickoff / now** | Jun 1 | Resourcing, design partner recruiting |

## Critical sequencing notes
- **Scope lock is the highest-leverage decision.** With ~6 weeks of real build time, ruthlessly cut to a "demoable, credible" v2. Park the rest as a fast-follow roadmap (which itself is great SaaStr messaging).
- **Recruit design partners in week 1**, not later — a 4-week beta needs committed users lined up before you build.
- **Marketing and GTM run in parallel**, not after engineering. Start booth/demo planning by early August.
- Build in a **1-week buffer** before Sept 7; something will slip.

## What I need from you to tighten this
1. **Exact SaaStr date** and whether "launch" = booth demo, press release, GA availability, or all three.
2. **Team size / engineering capacity** — 6 weeks of build is tight or comfortable depending on this.
3. **Is v2 net-new or an upgrade** to existing customers? (Changes the beta + migration work significantly.)

Want me to break any phase into a detailed sprint-level plan, or draft the scope-lock decision framework first?
